mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS rate transmits to equities through a discount-rate channel: when policy rates are falling, the present value of future S&P 500 earnings rises and 20-day forward returns are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand expectations, so S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next month when unemployment is accelerating.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 50 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad final-demand strength and resilient housing activity, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition on RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform single-series baselines, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of jointly strong episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Falling INDPRO reflects an industrial production slowdown that precedes weaker cyclical profits and lower S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal's rolling IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation disinflation combined with a still-restrictive fed funds rate implies real policy remains tight; when CPIAUCSL growth slows while FEDFUNDS stays elevated, equity multiples should compress and 20-day returns should be negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation over 3 months is falling
failure_condition: Reject if the combined disinflation-plus-tight-policy state does not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the direction reverses when CPIAUCSL slows further.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth raises expected corporate revenue and margins, so above-trend GDPC1 readings should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth is positive and accelerates versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend GDPC1 readings fail to coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the forward-return differential versus weak-growth periods is not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth stalls while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating faster than headline employment suggests, and S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next week as recession odds are repriced.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is nonpositive and UNRATE rises in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-deterioration trigger does not produce negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if returns are indistinguishable from zero after controlling for each series separately.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in retail sales growth signals weaker consumer spending and revenue momentum, so S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS contracts from recent highs.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines for 2 consecutive months or falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS contraction episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not materially below the unconditional benchmark.
